name: Magaz and Baltanás El Cerrato Loop
name_original: Magaz de Pisuerga desde Baltanas – circular por El sabinar
description: This moderate 31.2-mile road cycling route from Baltanás explores the El Cerrato region, featuring the Church of San Juan Bautista.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Magaz De Pisuerga traverse a varied landscape, from the gentle hills and expansive fields of the Palencia Cerrato plains to the more challenging terrain approaching the Palencia Mountain Nature Park. The region is characterized by agricultural areas, river valleys, and the historic Canal de Castilla, offering diverse backdrops for cyclists. Well-paved surfaces and dedicated paths contribute to the cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Magaz De Pisuerga The most popular road cycling route is Magaz and Baltanás El Cerrato Loop, a 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route explores the plains of El Cerrato. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puente Mayor de Palencia – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Tercer Barrio, a moderate 42.6 miles (68.6 km) path, offering views along the Pisuerga river. Local road cyclists also love the Fuente Juan José Niño – Nice place for picnic loop from Soto de Cerrato, a 28.1 miles (45.2 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Road cycling around Magaz De Pisuerga is defined by the Palencia Cerrato plains, the Pisuerga river, and the historic Canal de Castilla. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat, tranquil paths to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Magaz De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Magaz De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: There are over 40 dedicated road cycling routes around Magaz de Pisuerga, catering to various skill levels. These include 10 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 3 challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: The region offers diverse terrain. To the south, you'll find the gentle hills and expansive fields of the Palencia Cerrato plains. Heading north, the landscape transitions to the more rugged Palencia Mountain Nature Park, featuring significant elevation gains. The historic Canal de Castilla also provides flat, tranquil routes along its banks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: Yes, Magaz de Pisuerga offers several family-friendly road cycling options, particularly along the Canal de Castilla, which provides flat and tranquil paths. Additionally, there are 10 routes classified as 'easy' that are suitable for less experienced riders or families.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling in the area?
A: While cycling, you can explore various points of interest. In Magaz de Pisuerga itself, you can see the remains of the historic Castle of Magaz and the San Mamés Church with its 12th-century Romanesque apse. Nearby, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3166451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Grande de Palencia&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Palencia&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5793615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Puentecillas&lt;/a&gt;. The Canal de Castilla is also a significant historical feature.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: The region is enjoyable for road cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer provides warm, sunny conditions, ideal for early morning or late afternoon rides, while the plains transform with golden hues. The Palencia Mountain Nature Park can be more challenging in colder months but offers stunning views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170653750/magaz-and-baltanas-el-cerrato-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaz and Baltanás El Cerrato Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route exploring the plains. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Tercer Barrio&lt;/a&gt;, offering views along the Pisuerga river.

Q: What is the difficulty level of road cycling routes in this region?
A: The routes around Magaz de Pisuerga cater to all levels. You can find easy, flat paths, especially along the Canal de Castilla. The majority of routes are moderate, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Juan José Niño – Nice place for picnic loop from Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking a challenge, routes heading towards the Palencia Mountain Nature Park offer significant elevation gains and are classified as difficult.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: The road cycling routes in Magaz de Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 1,100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the well-paved surfaces and diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. While cycling towards the Palencia Mountain Nature Park, you'll encounter breathtaking panoramic viewpoints such as Piedrasluengas, Alto de la Varga, and Alba. Even on the plains, the changing agricultural landscapes provide picturesque vistas, especially during spring and summer.

Q: Are there options for longer road cycling tours from Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for endurance cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33894771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Cevico de la Torre loop from Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt; covers over 90 kilometers, offering a substantial ride through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What are the options for parking when starting a road cycling route in Magaz de Pisuerga?
A: Magaz de Pisuerga and surrounding towns typically offer public parking options suitable for cyclists. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, but generally, you can find convenient spots to begin your ride.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with exploring nearby cities?
A: Yes, Magaz de Pisuerga is conveniently located a short distance from Palencia City, which offers additional attractions like its impressive Cathedral and the Parque del Salón de Isabel II. You can plan routes that pass through or near Palencia, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38255247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercado de Abastos de Palencia – Puente Mayor de Palencia loop from Tercer Barrio&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to combine cycling with urban exploration.
tips: Baltanás is a small town famous for the underground wine storage cellars that can be found scattered up a hill in the town.

Magaz de Pisuerga is located at an important crossroads of the Pisuerga river. The town has remains of an 11th century castle destroyed by the French. Cycle past the 12th-century church and have a coffee in the Plaza Mayor.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It's a road cycling route in an area known for long distances without steep slopes, making it suitable for cyclists looking to accumulate kilometers on relatively gentle or rolling terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Baltanás. While specific parking details aren't provided, Baltanás is the recommended starting point, and you should be able to find suitable parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes by a couple of notable spots. You can see the Baltanás Underground Wine Cellars and the Church of San Juan Bautista in Magaz de Pisuerga. The name also suggests it passes through or near 'El Sabinar', which likely refers to a juniper forest, offering distinctive natural scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Palencia region offers diverse cycling opportunities. Generally, spring and autumn are ideal for road cycling in this area, providing pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather than summer or winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route.

faqs.question: Does this route pass through El Cerrato, and are there any specific regulations?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is located within the El Cerrato region. While there are no specific permits or fees for the cycling route itself, always respect local regulations regarding road safety, private property, and environmental protection when cycling through any natural area.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, it is generally suitable for cycling with dogs if they are accustomed to riding in a trailer or basket. However, dogs are not typically allowed to run alongside cyclists on public roads for safety reasons.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized as a moderate road cycling route. The area around Baltanás is known for long routes without steep slopes, suggesting a relatively gentle or rolling landscape, ideal for accumulating kilometers on a race bike.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You will briefly cross parts of the Ruta de Carlos V, Paso de ganado, Senda del sabinar de Soto, Calle Corrillo, Camino Soto - Monte - Reinoso, Camino de Soto a las Derrumbadas, and Camino de la Colorada.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and the fact it's a road cycling route, it might be suitable for families with older, experienced children who are comfortable with road cycling and the distance involved. For younger children, it might be too long or challenging.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects Baltanás and Magaz de Pisuerga. Both towns are likely to offer cafes, restaurants, or pubs where you can refuel or take a break during your ride.
